pr.title: run permuter on all remaining low-mismatch functions
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Broad decomp-matching cleanup pass over 34 files, driven by a script that sorted remaining functions by mismatch count and then ran a permuter/Codex workflow on low-mismatch functions. The merged changes are mostly small source rearrangements, type/name refinements, inline extraction, include pruning, register-allocation nudges, literal spelling changes, and a few global/struct layout fixes across fighter, game-mode, stage, menu/interface, camera, library, map, player, and VI code. The author explicitly reverted matches that depended on data-layout changes such as replacing inline strings with explicit variables, arguing those should wait until the whole translation unit is ready to link.
what_changed: Applied a large set of low-mismatch permuter wins: 330 added lines and 286 deleted lines across 34 changed files.

In camera pause-camera code, introduced a distinct local named selected_slot and rewrote chained float initialization as zoom_dir = x_val = y_val = 0.0f, affecting Camera_8002CB0C register/expression shape.
In ftCo_800AFC40, extracted the item/x1A88.x4C and x1A88.x50 setup into static inline ftCo_800AFC40_inline0, removing an extra local from the main function body while preserving the field writes.
In ftCo_Bury, added static inline ftCo_Bury_GetTranslate returning &fp->mv.co.bury.translate, then used it as the first argument to PSVECAdd to influence generated code shape.

In ftCo_ItemThrow.c, wrapped ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys in pragma push/global_optimizer off/pop, added a deliberate empty cd != NULL branch comment for register allocation, and reordered the multiplication expression for cd->x404, gr_friction, and cd->x40C.

In ftKb_Init.c, removed a large set of now-unneeded includes and changed multiple Kirby special-N hat callbacks from the generic ftCo_8009D704 call to more specific ftCo_8009D4D4, ftCo_8009D074, ftCo_8009D2A4, ftCo_8009D920, ftCo_8009D5EC, ftCo_8009D18C, ftCo_8009D3BC, and ftCo_8009DA38 calls.
In ftaction.c, replaced repeated (1 / 256.0f) expressions with the decimal literal 0.003906f for command offset/range/hitbox/charge scaling.
In ftcpuattack.c, changed HSD_ASSERT(0x28A, NULL) to HSD_ASSERT(0x28A, 0), likely for exact constant form matching.
In gm_1601.c, changed a local val in gm_IncrementPowerCount from u32 to s32.
In gm_16AE.c, split one temporary pointer into dst and copied_dst names and routed subsequent struct copy/call uses through copied_dst, shaping allocation and lifetimes in fn_8016D634.

In gm_1832.c, introduced gm_1832_8047368C_t with STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x24), declared extern lbl_8047368C, switched game_type reads from lbl_804735E8.xE8 to lbl_8047368C.game_type, and named the lbl_803D9498 entry type for indexed transform data.

In gm_18A5.c, converted lbl_804DA78C from a local static initialized word to an extern .sdata2 symbol and renamed several permuter-slop locals to descriptive names such as created_text, created_text2, color_word, text_color_word, and text_color.
smart_moves: Used mismatch-count sorting to prioritize functions where automated permutation was likely to yield cheap matches.
Kept the PR broad but constrained to low-mismatch source-level changes, making it useful as a harvesting pass rather than a deep rewrite of one subsystem.

Explicitly rejected data-layout-based matches, especially swaps from inline strings to explicitly defined variables, until the affected translation unit is ready to link; this avoids prematurely baking in possibly wrong layout assumptions.

Preferred small local-expression and lifetime changes over semantic-looking rewrites: local renames, pointer aliases, literal form changes, and inline helper extraction were used to influence codegen without claiming new behavior.
Used a targeted pragma/global_optimizer off block only around ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys where a register-allocation issue was documented in a comment.
Pruned excessive includes in ftKb_Init.c as part of the matching pass, reducing header noise while preserving required declarations.
Added a size-asserted struct typedef for lbl_8047368C instead of continuing to access a possibly incorrect offset through lbl_804735E8, making a data-layout hypothesis explicit and checkable.
decomp_lessons: For low-mismatch functions, automated permuter searches can find many last-mile matches if functions are sorted by mismatch count and reviewed manually afterward.
Do not accept matches that depend primarily on speculative global/string/data-layout movement unless the whole translation unit is ready to link; codegen wins can otherwise hide layout debt.
Tiny spelling differences in floating constants can matter: 0.003906f may match where (1 / 256.0f) does not.
NULL versus integer 0 in assertions can affect matching even when semantically equivalent.
Introducing or splitting local pointer aliases can shape register allocation and lifetime enough to close mismatches.
Static inline helpers are useful for forcing an address computation or field-access sequence while keeping the source structured.
A harmless-looking empty branch can be retained when it is explicitly needed for register allocation, but should be commented as such.
Pragmas such as global_optimizer off should be narrowly scoped with push/pop around the function requiring them.
When a global is suspected to live in .sdata2, declaring it as extern with an address comment may be preferable to defining a static initializer in the wrong place.
Replacing anonymous static array element structs with named typedefs can make repeated indexed field access cleaner and can help guide codegen.
Include pruning can be part of matching work, especially when a file has accumulated broad transitive dependencies.
naming_conventions: New inline helper names followed the local function-address style, e.g. ftCo_800AFC40_inline0.
Small accessors used descriptive domain names, e.g. ftCo_Bury_GetTranslate for returning &fp->mv.co.bury.translate.
New struct typedefs used file/function or global-address context, e.g. gm_1832_8047368C_t and gm_80186F6C_Entry.

Per PR diff, generic permuter variables like new_var/new_var2/new_var3/new_var4/new_var5 were replaced with semantic names such as color_word, created_text, created_text2, text_color_word, and text_color.
In Camera_8002CB0C, valid was replaced by selected_slot, making the value's role clearer.
In gm_16AE.c, temp_r6 was split into dst and copied_dst, indicating pointer role/lifetime rather than raw register identity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Run a permuter over functions ordered by mismatch count to find high-probability last-mile matches.
Use chained assignments to control initialization order/code shape, as in zoom_dir = x_val = y_val = 0.0f.
Change arithmetic association/order, e.g. (cd->x404 * fp->co_attrs.gr_friction) * cd->x40C instead of cd->x40C * (...), to match floating-point instruction order.
Use exact literal spelling such as 0.003906f instead of an expression like 1 / 256.0f.
Introduce extra pointer aliases or split aliases to alter register allocation and lifetime.
Extract small static inline helpers to force a particular call-site expression or address calculation.
Use #pragma push, #pragma global_optimizer off, and #pragma pop around a single difficult function when optimizer behavior prevents a match.
Add an otherwise empty conditional guarded by a comment when needed solely for register allocation.
Use integer 0 instead of NULL in macros if the original codegen expects an integer literal form.
Make suspected global layout explicit with extern declarations and STATIC_ASSERT-sized structs instead of anonymous offset access.
Remove or reduce includes when broader header exposure is unnecessary after the matched source is clarified.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The author noted in the PR body that data-layout-related matches, such as swapping inline strings for explicitly-defined variables, were reverted and should wait until the whole translation unit is ready to link.
The author characterized the mismatch-sorted permuter workflow as fruitful and planned to continue using it for easy wins.
searchable_terms: run permuter on all remaining low-mismatch functions
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs by lukechampine continuing the mismatch-count permuter workflow.
Search for functions still using data-layout-related workarounds that were deferred until full TU linking.
Search for #pragma global_optimizer off to audit narrowly scoped register-allocation fixes.
Search for ftCo_800AFC40_inline0 and ftCo_Bury_GetTranslate to see whether inline helpers survived later cleanup.
Search for lbl_8047368C and gm_1832_8047368C_t to validate the struct layout against later game-mode work.
Search for remaining (1 / 256.0f) expressions that might need literal-form matching.
